title: Flower Queen

{{Quote|What? It's just you? Ah!|The Flower Queen|<ref name="tss" />}}
The '''Flower Queen''' was a being who lived on the [[Endor/Legends|Forest Moon of Endor]]. She and her siblings, the [[Sun King]], [[Leaf Queen]], and [[Snow King]], were responsible for controlling the weather of Endor using the [[season scepter]], but in [[3 ABY/Legends|3 ABY]], the Snow King fell under the influence of a nefarious [[Frost Sprite]] named [[Odra]], who led him to freeze over the entire [[Moon/Legends|moon]]. The remaining seasonal beings tried to enlist [[Ewok/Legends|Ewok]] [[Shaman/Legends|shaman]] [[Logray/Legends|Logray]] for assistance, but several [[Wokling/Legends|young]] Ewoks came instead, much to the Flower Queen's dismay. However, the Ewoks were able to steal back the [[Scepter/Legends|scepter]], saving Endor from a bitter [[Winter/Legends|winter]].

==Biography==
{{Quote|No sunstar? Then only the Season Scepter can melt my brother's heart, and we don't have it. Oh, no.|The Flower Queen|<ref name="tss" />}}
The Flower Queen was a powerful [[Gender/Legends|female]] being who resided on the [[Endor/Legends|Forest Moon of Endor]]. She worked with the [[Sun King]], [[Leaf Queen]], and [[Snow King]]—her siblings—to control the weather of the [[Moon/Legends|moon]] using the [[season scepter]],<ref name="tss" /> a [[The Force/Legends|Force]]-powered [[Staff/Legends|staff]].<ref name="castaways">{{Hyperspace|url=fans/hyperspace/source/feature20081201.html|text=Castaways of Endor|int=Castaways of Endor}}</ref> In [[3 ABY/Legends|3 ABY]],<ref name="Date">{{EwoksDate|the appearance of the Flower Queen|[[The Season Scepter]]}}</ref> the Snow King fell in love with the nefarious [[Frost Sprite]] [[Odra]], whose influence chilled the [[King/Legends|king's]] [[Heart/Legends|heart]] and led him to inflict an intense [[Water/Legends|snowy]] season upon Endor. In response, the Leaf Queen called for the [[Ewok/Legends|Ewok]] [[Shaman/Legends|shaman]] [[Logray/Legends|Logray's]] help, but as he was unavailable, several [[Wokling/Legends|young Ewoks]] came instead: [[Wicket Wystri Warrick/Legends|Wicket Warrick]], [[Kneesaa a Jari Kintaka/Legends|Kneesaa a Jari Kintaka]], [[Teebo/Legends|Teebo]], and [[Latara/Legends|Latara]].<ref name="tss" />

[[File:FlowerQueenMeeting-TSS.png|thumb|left|275px|The Flower Queen (far left) meets with her siblings and the Ewoks regarding the Odra crisis.]]
They arrived at the [[Sun King's palace]] via a [[flying leaf]] the Leaf Queen had sent, though Kintaka had been captured by the Snow King's [[Icehead]]s on the way there. The Leaf Queen introduced herself and her siblings, with the Flower Queen fretting about how her [[Flower/Legends|petals]] were not yet [[Color/Legends|colored]] properly for the season. The Leaf Queen explained how an early [[Winter/Legends|winter]] had befallen Endor, which comforted Teebo, who was under the impression that he had caused the snow by a mistaken [[Magic/Legends|magic]] spell. The Ewoks then revealed that neither Logray nor his magical [[Gemstone/Legends|gem]], the [[sunstar]], were with them, which stressed out the Flower Queen. She worriedly explained that the only solution would be to unfreeze the Snow King's heart with the [[Scepter/Legends|scepter]].<ref name="tss" />

The remaining Ewoks hurried to the [[Snow Palace]], where Kintaka was being held, and successfully recovered the scepter, freed Kintaka, unfroze the Snow King, and melted Odra down to miniature size. By the [[Time/Legends|time]] they were victorious, Logray, [[Chief/Legends|Chief]] [[Chirpa/Legends|Chirpa]], the Flower Queen, the Sun King, and the Leaf Queen arrived, only to find that the situation was under control.<ref name="tss" /> [[Standard year/Legends|Years]] later, [[Professor/Legends|Professor]] [[Mankuskett]], [[Chair of Metagrowth and Polymorphism]] at the [[University of Sanbra]], described in a [[12 ABY/Legends|12 ABY]] paper that magic on Endor had turned "local kingpins into weather-controlling warlocks."<ref name="castaways" />

==Personality and traits==
{{Quote|Oh, I'm just not ready. Oh, why my petals aren't even colored yet. Oh, no, no.|The Flower Queen|<ref name="tss" />}}
The Flower Queen was the most worried among her siblings during the [[Odra crisis]], both about how they would successfully defeat Odra and about her own petals' appearance. She had black [[Eye/Legends|eyes]] and dark yellow skin, with a lighter shade of yellow protruding from the inside of her petals. Her torso was green in the middle and covered in pink on the sides and [[Limb/Legends|limbs]].<ref name="tss" />

==Behind the scenes==
The Flower Queen first appeared in "[[The Season Scepter]],"<ref name="tss" /> the thirteenth episode of the [[Ewoks: Season Two|second season]] of the animated television series [[Ewoks (television series)|''Ewoks'']], which premiered on [[November 1]], [[1986]].<ref name="Insider 27">{{InsiderCite|27|A Star Wars CELibration|A ''Star Wars'' CELibration}}</ref>
